info.title: **Cleaning up duplicate processes in btop: diagnosis and optimization**

parts.text: [Image 1] Why are there so many duplicate processes when i run btop? Is there anything you can do to optimise this?

**Planning process inspection**

I'm currently in planning mode, and I need to inspect processes. I can't run certain commands in read-only mode, but maybe I need to get some skills for diagnostics and optimization. My next step is to draft a plan and perhaps ask for approval before implementing anything. First, I'll make sure to update what I have so far. This approach should help clarify things!
I’ll inspect the active process tree and the commands behind those repeated `bash`, `rg`, `ocv`, and `2.1.144` entries without changing anything, then I’ll propose the smallest safe optimisations.

input.description: Inspect active processes by CPU usage

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: # Wrap a Claude Code hook command with timeout fallback and failure logging.
3: #
4: # macOS lacks GNU `timeout` by default. Without coreutils installed (which
5: # provides `gtimeout`), every hook command prefixed `timeout 60 ...` failed
6: # silently because hooks also use `2>/dev/null || true`. This wrapper:
7: #   1. Picks `timeout`, then `gtimeout`, else runs the command bare.
8: #   2. Logs non-zero exits to ~/.claude/logs/hooks/<date>.log so future
9: #      regressions are visible instead of buried.
10: #   3. Always exits 0 so Claude Code's hook lifecycle is never blocked.
11: #
12: # Usage: hook-run.sh <secs> <command> [args...]
13: set -u
14: 
15: secs="${1:-60}"
16: shift || true
17: 
18: log_dir="${HOME}/.claude/logs/hooks"
19: mkdir -p "$log_dir" 2>/dev/null || true
20: log_file="${log_dir}/$(date +%Y-%m-%d).log"
21: 
22: if command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
23:     runner=(timeout "$secs")
24: elif command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
25:     runner=(gtimeout "$secs")
26: else
27:     runner=()
28: fi
29: 
30: output=$("${runner[@]}" "$@" 2>&1)
31: rc=$?
32: 
33: if [[ $rc -ne 0 ]]; then
34:     {
35:         printf '[%s] rc=%d cmd=' "$(date -u +%FT%TZ)" "$rc"
36:         printf '%q ' "$@"
37:         printf '\n'
38:         printf '%s\n---\n' "$output" | head -25
39:     } >> "$log_file" 2>/dev/null || true
40: fi
41: 
42: exit 0

(End of file - total 42 lines)

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Wrap a Claude Code hook command with timeout fallback and failure logging.
#
# macOS lacks GNU `timeout` by default. Without coreutils installed (which
# provides `gtimeout`), every hook command prefixed `timeout 60 ...` failed
# silently because hooks also use `2>/dev/null || true`. This wrapper:
#   1. Picks `timeout`, then `gtimeout`, else runs the command bare.
#   2. Logs non-zero exits to ~/.claude/logs/hooks/<date>.log so future
#      regressions are visible instead of buried.
#   3. Always exits 0 so Claude Code's hook lifecycle is never blocked.
#
# Usage: hook-run.sh <secs> <command> [args...]
set -u

secs="${1:-60}"
shift || true

log_dir="${HOME}/.claude/logs/hooks"
mkdir -p "$log_dir" 2>/dev/null || true
log_file="${log_dir}/$(date +%Y-%m-%d).log"

Instructions from: /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/.claude/AGENTS.md
# AGENTS.md - Claude Code Agent System Reference

Specialized agent system for Claude Code with domain, project-specific, and compact-output subagent files.

## Shell Reminder

- Fish is the primary shell for this machine.
- Do not suggest `unset` for interactive shell usage; use `set -e VAR` in Fish.
- `env -u VAR command` is acceptable for one-off commands started from Fish.
- If an example requires Bash or Zsh syntax, label it explicitly.

## Overview

Each agent is a **Claude Code subagent file** (`.claude/agents/*.md`) with YAML frontmatter defining name, description, tool access, model, and optional features. Claude auto-delegates to subagents based on their description fields. Each subagent runs in its own context window with a custom system prompt.

**Core Features**:
- **Subagent Files**: Markdown files with YAML frontmatter in `.claude/agents/`
- **Auto-Delegation**: Claude uses description fields to decide when to delegate
- **Tool Restrictions**: Each agent has specific tool access (read-only vs full)
- **Model Selection**: `haiku` for fast read-only agents, `inherit` for full capability
- **Persistent Memory**: `memory: project` on architect for cross-session learning
- **Background Mode**: `background: true` on test-runner for concurrent execution
- **Cost Control**: `maxTurns` on bounded agents (test-runner, dotfiles-doctor, mentor)
- **Skill Preloading**: `skills` field injects skill content at startup (shell-expert)
- **MCP Access**: `mcpServers: deepwiki` on architect and mentor for repo documentation
- **Lifecycle Hooks**: SubagentStart/SubagentStop events in settings.json for logging
- **Manual Override**: Use `--persona-[name]` flags for explicit control
- **Flag Integration**: Works with all thinking flags, MCP servers, and command categories

**Frontmatter Fields** (from [official docs](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents)):

| Field | Required | Description |
|-------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | Yes | Agent identifier (must match filename) |
| `description` | Yes | When to delegate (Claude reads this) |
| `tools` | No | Comma-separated allowed tools |
| `disallowedTools` | No | Comma-separated blocked tools |
| `model` | No | `sonnet`, `opus`, `haiku`, or `inherit` (default) |
| `memory` | No | `user`, `project`, or `local` persistence scope |
| `background` | No | `true` for concurrent execution |
| `maxTurns` | No | Limit API round-trips (cost control) |
| `skills` | No | Comma-separated skills to preload |
| `mcpServers` | No | Comma-separated MCP servers to enable |
| `hooks` | No | Agent-scoped hooks (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop) |
| `isolation` | No | `worktree` for isolated git worktree |
| `permissionMode` | No | Permission level override |

## Available Agents

### Technical Specialists

| Agent | Flag | Description | Primary Use Cases |
|-------|------|-------------|-------------------|
| [architect](agents/architect.md) | `--persona-architect` | Systems architecture specialist | System design, scalability, long-term planning |
| [frontend](agents/frontend.md) | `--persona-frontend` | UX specialist & accessibility advocate | UI components, responsive design, user experience |
| [backend](agents/backend.md) | `--persona-backend` | Reliability engineer & API specialist | Server development, APIs, data integrity |
| [security](agents/security.md) | `--persona-security` | Threat modeler & vulnerability specialist | Security audits, threat analysis, hardening |
| [performance](agents/performance.md) | `--persona-performance` | Optimization & bottleneck specialist | Performance tuning, metrics, optimization |

### Process & Quality Experts

| Agent | Flag | Description | Primary Use Cases |
|-------|------|-------------|-------------------|
| [analyzer](agents/analyzer.md) | `--persona-analyzer` | Root cause & investigation specialist | Debugging, troubleshooting, analysis |
| [qa](agents/qa.md) | `--persona-qa` | Quality advocate & testing specialist | Testing, validation, quality assurance |
| [refactorer](agents/refactorer.md) | `--persona-refactorer` | Code quality & technical debt manager | Code cleanup, refactoring, simplification |
| [devops](agents/devops.md) | `--persona-devops` | Infrastructure & automation specialist | CI/CD, deployment, infrastructure |
| [devops-security-auditor](agents/devops-security-auditor.md) | Special | DevOps security assessment specialist | Infrastructure security, container security |

### Knowledge & Communication

| Agent | Flag | Description | Primary Use Cases |
|-------|------|-------------|-------------------|
| [mentor](agents/mentor.md) | `--persona-mentor` | Educational & knowledge transfer specialist | Teaching, documentation, explanations |
| [scribe](agents/scribe.md) | `--persona-scribe=lang` | Documentation & localization specialist | Professional writing, documentation, translation |

### Project-Specific Agents

| Agent | Model | Extra Features | Primary Use Cases |
|-------|-------|----------------|-------------------|
| [shell-expert](agents/shell-expert.md) | inherit | `skills: fish-reload, dotfiles-sync` | Shell functions, scripts, Fish/Zsh parity |
| [test-runner](agents/test-runner.md) | haiku | `background: true`, `maxTurns: 10` | Running test suites, reporting results |
| [dotfiles-doctor](agents/dotfiles-doctor.md) | haiku | `maxTurns: 15` | Stow validation, tool checks, theme consistency |

### Compact Caveman Agents

Use these when the task has a known shape and the parent context needs operational signal without prose. Do not use them for open-ended architecture, security threat modeling, mentoring, or requirements discovery.

| Agent | Model | Extra Features | Primary Use Cases |
|-------|-------|----------------|-------------------|
| [caveman-investigator](agents/caveman-investigator.md) | haiku | `maxTurns: 8` | Root-cause evidence, file location, compact debug handoff |
| [caveman-reviewer](agents/caveman-reviewer.md) | inherit | `maxTurns: 10` | Findings-only review with severity, file:line, fix |
| [caveman-tester](agents/caveman-tester.md) | haiku | `background: true`, `maxTurns: 8` | Targeted validation receipts |
| [caveman-builder](agents/caveman-builder.md) | inherit | `maxTurns: 12` | Surgical execution of already-defined small changes |
| [caveman-checklist](agents/caveman-checklist.md) | haiku | `maxTurns: 6` | Terse execution checklist for already-scoped work |

## Agent Activation

### Manual Activation
Use flags to explicitly activate agents:
```bash
--persona-architect           # Activate architect agent
--persona-frontend --persona-qa  # Activate multiple agents
--persona-scribe=es          # Activate scribe with Spanish language
```

### Auto-Activation
Agents activate automatically based on:
- **Keywords**: Domain-specific terms trigger relevant agents
- **Context**: Project type and current task determine agent selection
- **Commands**: Specific commands have preferred agents
- **Complexity**: High complexity triggers analytical agents

### Command Integration
Commands work seamlessly with agents:
```bash
/build --persona-frontend     # Frontend-focused build
/analyze                       # Auto-activates analyzer, architect, security
/improve --persona-refactorer # Refactoring-focused improvements
/test --persona-qa            # QA-focused testing
```

## Cross-Agent Collaboration

Agents can work together for complex tasks:

### Common Collaboration Patterns
- **architect + performance**: System design with performance optimization
- **security + backend**: Secure server-side development
- **frontend + qa**: User-focused development with testing
- **mentor + scribe**: Educational content creation
- **analyzer + refactorer**: Root cause analysis with code improvement
- **devops + security**: Infrastructure automation with security

### Conflict Resolution
When multiple agents are active:
1. **Primary Agent**: Leads decision-making within domain
2. **Consulting Agents**: Provide specialized input
3. **Validation Agents**: Review decisions for quality
4. **Priority Matrix**: Resolves conflicts using agent hierarchies

## Flag Integration

All agents work with the complete flag system:

### Thinking Flags
- `--think`: Enhanced analysis (4K tokens)
- `--think-hard`: Deep analysis (10K tokens)
- `--ultrathink`: Maximum analysis (32K tokens)

### MCP Server Flags
- `--seq`: Sequential reasoning
- `--c7`: Context7 documentation
- `--magic`: UI component generation
- `--play`: Playwright testing

### Optimization Flags
- `--uc`: Ultra-compressed output
- `--validate`: Pre-operation validation
- `--safe-mode`: Conservative execution
- `--loop`: Iterative improvement

### Orchestration Flags
- `--wave-mode`: Multi-stage execution
- `--delegate`: Sub-agent delegation
- `--concurrency`: Parallel processing

## Agent Selection Guide

### By Task Type
- **Building**: frontend, backend, architect
- **Debugging**: analyzer, qa, performance
- **Security**: security, devops-security-auditor
- **Documentation**: scribe, mentor
- **Optimization**: performance, refactorer
- **Infrastructure**: devops, architect

### By Project Phase
- **Planning**: architect, analyzer
- **Implementation**: frontend, backend
- **Testing**: qa, performance
- **Deployment**: devops, security
- **Maintenance**: refactorer, analyzer

### By Complexity
- **Simple tasks**: Single specialized agent
- **Moderate tasks**: Primary + consulting agent
- **Complex tasks**: Multiple collaborative agents
- **Critical tasks**: Full agent team with validation

## Configuration

Agents respect all system configurations:
- Token limits and compression settings
- MCP server availability
- Resource management thresholds
- Quality gate requirements
- Validation cycles

## Best Practices

1. **Let auto-activation work**: The system usually selects the right agents
2. **Use explicit flags for precision**: Override when you need specific expertise
3. **Combine agents for complex tasks**: Multiple perspectives improve outcomes
4. **Trust agent expertise**: Each agent excels in their domain
5. **Monitor agent recommendations**: Agents provide domain-specific insights

## Quick Reference

```bash
# Single agent activation
--persona-frontend

# Multiple agents
--persona-architect --persona-security

# Agent with language
--persona-scribe=fr

# Agent with other flags
--persona-performance --think-hard --uc

# Command with agent
/build --persona-backend --validate
```

For detailed information about each agent, click on the agent name in the tables above to view their full specification.

## Agent Teams Integration

The persona-based agent system complements Claude Code's native **Agent Teams** feature (experimental). Agent Teams spawn separate Claude Code instances that communicate via peer-to-peer messaging, while personas operate within a single session.

### When to Use Each
| Scenario | Best Approach |
|----------|--------------|
| Single-session domain expertise | Personas (`--persona-*` flags) |
| Parallel same-repo collaboration | Agent Teams (teammates with shared task list) |
| Isolated multi-branch work | `gwt-parallel` (devcontainer + worktree per branch) |
| Autonomous ticket execution | `gwt-ticket` + OpenCode + nvim |

### Combining Personas with Agent Teams
Teammates spawned via Agent Teams load CLAUDE.md, so persona auto-activation works within each teammate's session. When creating teams, leverage this by assigning domain-specific context:
```
Spawn a teammate focused on security review (will auto-activate security persona)
Spawn a teammate for frontend components (will auto-activate frontend persona)
```

1: #!/usr/bin/env python3
2: """
3: SessionEnd Hook - Extract memories from completed Claude Code session.
4: 
5: This hook triggers when a Claude Code session ends and:
6: 1. Parses the session transcript
7: 2. Detects memory triggers (corrections, enthusiasm, recovery patterns)
8: 3. Calls Claude to extract memories
9: 4. Writes memories directly to Obsidian vault
10: 
11: Hook Input (stdin JSON):
12: {
13:   "hook_type": "SessionEnd",
14:   "session_id": "abc123",
15:   "cwd": "/path/to/project",
16:   "transcript_path": "/path/to/transcript.txt"
17: }
18: 
19: Hook Output (stdout JSON):
20: {
21:   "continue": true
22: }
23: """
24: 
25: import json
26: import subprocess
27: import sys
28: import re
29: import os
30: from pathlib import Path
31: from datetime import datetime
32: from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional
33: 
34: # Add lib to path
35: sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent))
36: 
37: from lib.config import (
38:     CLAUDE_PROJECTS_DIR, SESSIONS_DIR, MEMORIES_DIR,
39:     TRIGGER_PATTERNS, FAILURE_PATTERNS, SUCCESS_PATTERNS,
40:     MAX_MEMORIES_PER_SESSION, MAX_TRANSCRIPT_LENGTH, MEMORY_TYPES
41: )
42: from lib.markdown_writer import write_session, write_memory, update_frontmatter
43: from lib.ripgrep_search import find_session_by_id
44: 
45: 
46: def detect_triggers(messages: List[str]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
47:     """
48:     Detect memory extraction triggers in user messages.
49: 
50:     Returns list of detected triggers with type, priority, and context.
51:     """
52:     triggers = []
53: 
54:     for i, message in enumerate(messages):
55:         for trigger_name, config in TRIGGER_PATTERNS.items():
56:             for pattern in config['patterns']:
57:                 match = re.search(pattern, message, re.IGNORECASE)
58:                 if match:
59:                     triggers.append({
60:                         'type': trigger_name,
61:                         'message_index': i,
62:                         'message': message[:200],
63:                         'match': match.group(0),
64:                         'priority': config['priority'],
65:                         'suggested_type': config['memory_type']
66:                     })
67:                     break  # One match per trigger type per message
68: 
69:     # Detect recovery patterns (fail -> succeed sequence)
70:     triggers.extend(detect_recovery_patterns(messages))
71: 
72:     return triggers
73: 
74: 
75: def detect_recovery_patterns(messages: List[str]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
76:     """Detect recovery patterns (multiple failures followed by success)."""
77:     triggers = []
78:     failure_count = 0
79:     last_failure_idx = -1
80: 
81:     for i, message in enumerate(messages):
82:         is_failure = any(re.search(p, message, re.IGNORECASE) for p in FAILURE_PATTERNS)
83:         is_success = any(re.search(p, message, re.IGNORECASE) for p in SUCCESS_PATTERNS)
84: 
85:         if is_failure:
86:             failure_count += 1
87:             last_failure_idx = i
88:         elif is_success and failure_count >= 1 and i - last_failure_idx <= 3:
89:             triggers.append({
90:                 'type': 'recovery_pattern',
91:                 'message_index': i,
92:                 'message': message[:200],
93:                 'match': f'Recovery after {failure_count} attempt(s)',
94:                 'priority': 'high',
95:                 'suggested_type': 'learning'
96:             })
97:             failure_count = 0
98: 
99:     return triggers
100: 
101: 
102: def parse_session_jsonl(session_id: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
103:     """
104:     Find and parse a Claude Code session JSONL file.
105: 
106:     Returns parsed session data or None if not found.
107:     """
108:     if not CLAUDE_PROJECTS_DIR.exists():
109:         return None
110: 
111:     # Search for session file
112:     for jsonl_file in CLAUDE_PROJECTS_DIR.rglob('*.jsonl'):
113:         try:
114:             with open(jsonl_file, 'r') as f:
115:                 first_line = f.readline()
116:                 if not first_line:
117:                     continue
118: 
119:                 data = json.loads(first_line)
120:                 if data.get('sessionId') == session_id:
121:                     # Found the session - parse it
122:                     f.seek(0)
123:                     return parse_jsonl_content(f.read(), jsonl_file)
124:         except Exception:
125:             continue
126: 
127:     return None
128: 
129: 
130: def parse_jsonl_content(content: str, file_path: Path) -> Dict[str, Any]:
131:     """Parse JSONL session content into structured data."""
132:     user_messages = []
133:     assistant_messages = []
134:     tool_calls = []
135:     files_touched = set()
136:     created_at = None
137:     session_id = None
138: 
139:     for line in content.strip().split('\n'):
140:         if not line.strip():
141:             continue
142: 
143:         try:
144:             data = json.loads(line)
145: 
146:             # Extract session metadata
147:             if not session_id:
148:                 session_id = data.get('sessionId', '')
149:             if not created_at and data.get('timestamp'):
150:                 created_at = data['timestamp']
151: 
152:             # Extract messages
153:             msg_type = data.get('type', '')
154: 
155:             if msg_type == 'user':
156:                 text = data.get('message', {}).get('content', '')
157:                 if isinstance(text, str) and text.strip():
158:                     user_messages.append(text)
159: 
160:             elif msg_type == 'assistant':
161:                 text = data.get('message', {}).get('content', '')
162:                 if isinstance(text, str):
163:                     assistant_messages.append(text[:500])  # Truncate
164: 
165:             elif msg_type == 'tool_use':
166:                 tool_name = data.get('name', '')
167:                 tool_input = data.get('input', {})
168: 
169:                 tool_calls.append({
170:                     'name': tool_name,
171:                     'input': tool_input
172:                 })
173: 
174:                 # Extract files from tool calls
175:                 if tool_name in ['Read', 'Edit', 'Write']:
176:                     file_path = tool_input.get('file_path', '')
177:                     if file_path:
178:                         files_touched.add(file_path)
179:                 elif tool_name == 'Glob':
180:                     pattern = tool_input.get('pattern', '')
181:                     if pattern:
182:                         files_touched.add(f'[glob: {pattern}]')
183: 
184:         except json.JSONDecodeError:
185:             continue
186: 
187:     # Classify work type
188:     work_type = classify_work_type(user_messages, tool_calls)
189: 
190:     # Estimate tokens
191:     total_text = ' '.join(user_messages + assistant_messages)
192:     token_estimate = len(total_text) // 4
193: 
194:     return {
195:         'session_id': session_id or file_path.stem,
196:         'created_at': created_at or datetime.now().isoformat(),
197:         'work_type': work_type,
198:         'user_messages': user_messages,
199:         'assistant_messages': assistant_messages,
200:         'tool_calls': tool_calls,
201:         'files_touched': list(files_touched),
202:         'token_estimate': token_estimate,
203:         'first_message': user_messages[0] if user_messages else '',
204:         'last_message': user_messages[-1] if user_messages else ''
205:     }
206: 
207: 
208: def classify_work_type(messages: List[str], tool_calls: List[Dict]) -> str:
209:     """Classify the type of work done in the session."""
210:     text = ' '.join(messages).lower()
211: 
212:     patterns = {
213:         'debugging': ['debug', 'fix', 'bug', 'error', 'issue', 'problem', 'broken'],
214:         'development': ['implement', 'create', 'build', 'add', 'feature', 'component'],
215:         'refactoring': ['refactor', 'cleanup', 'reorganize', 'restructure', 'rename'],
216:         'testing': ['test', 'spec', 'coverage', 'assertion', 'expect'],
217:         'documentation': ['document', 'readme', 'comment', 'explain', 'docs'],
218:         'research': ['research', 'explore', 'investigate', 'find', 'search', 'understand'],
219:         'planning': ['plan', 'design', 'architect', 'strategy', 'roadmap'],
220:     }

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
SessionEnd Hook - Extract memories from completed Claude Code session.

This hook triggers when a Claude Code session ends and:
1. Parses the session transcript
2. Detects memory triggers (corrections, enthusiasm, recovery patterns)
3. Calls Claude to extract memories
4. Writes memories directly to Obsidian vault

{
  "hook_type": "SessionEnd",
  "session_id": "abc123",
  "cwd": "/path/to/project",
  "transcript_path": "/path/to/transcript.txt"
}

1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: #
3: # session-synthesize.sh - Synthesize Claude Code session into Obsidian documentation
4: #
5: # Gathers session context (plan fields, changelog entries, git commits, beads)
6: # and uses claude --print to generate structured Obsidian documentation.
7: #
8: # Idempotent: uses the Claude session UUID (from JSONL filename) as the session
9: # ID. Falls back to md5(HEAD-commit + branch + project) when JSONL is unavailable.
10: # Atomic writes via temp file + mv. Replaces corrupt/incomplete existing files.
11: #
12: # Usage:
13: #   session-synthesize.sh [OPTIONS]
14: #   session-synthesize.sh --worktree /path/to/worktree
15: #   session-synthesize.sh --cwd /path/to/project
16: #   session-synthesize.sh --reconcile          # Catch-up missed sessions
17: #   session-synthesize.sh --dry-run
18: #
19: # Output:
20: #   Writes to ~/obsidian/Claude/Sessions/<date>-synth-<session-id>.md
21: #   Adds Obsidian wikilink in project folder (no file copies)
22: 
23: set -euo pipefail
24: 
25: # Configuration
26: OBSIDIAN_VAULT="${OBSIDIAN_VAULT:-$HOME/obsidian}"
27: CLAUDE_SESSIONS_DIR="$OBSIDIAN_VAULT/Claude/Sessions"
28: CLAUDE_PROJECTS_DIR="${HOME}/.claude/projects"
29: TODAY=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d)
30: MAX_PROMPT_BYTES=30000
31: 
32: # Colors
33: RED='\033[0;31m'
34: GREEN='\033[0;32m'
35: YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
36: BLUE='\033[0;34m'
37: NC='\033[0m'
38: 
39: # Defaults
40: DRY_RUN=false
41: WORKTREE_PATH=""
42: PROJECT_CWD=""
43: TICKET_ID=""
44: VERBOSE=false
45: RECONCILE=false
46: FORCE_SESSION_ID=""
47: SYNTH_REASON="${SYNTH_REASON:-manual}"
48: FORCE=false
49: DEDUP_SEC="${OBSIDIAN_SYNC_DEDUP_SEC:-60}"
50: DEDUP_DIR="${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/obsidian-sync-dedup"
51: 
52: show_help() {
53:     cat <<'EOF'
54: session-synthesize.sh - Synthesize Claude session into Obsidian
55: 
56: USAGE:
57:   session-synthesize.sh [OPTIONS]
58: 
59: OPTIONS:
60:   --worktree PATH   Worktree path (for gwt-ticket sessions)
61:   --cwd PATH        Project working directory (default: current dir)
62:   --ticket ID       Ticket/issue ID to associate
63:   --session-id UUID Force a specific session ID (used by reconcile)
64:   --reconcile       Catch up missed sessions (crash/SIGKILL recovery)
65:   --dry-run         Print synthesis prompt without writing
66:   --verbose         Show gathered context
67:   --help            Show this help
68: 
69: ENVIRONMENT:
70:   OBSIDIAN_VAULT    Path to Obsidian vault (default: ~/obsidian)
71: 
72: WHAT IT DOES:
73:   1. Resolves a unique session ID (Claude JSONL UUID or content hash)
74:   2. If valid output file already exists, exits (idempotent)
75:   3. Extracts structured fields from session context sources
76:   4. Redacts secrets from all extracted content
77:   5. Calls claude --print to generate documentation
78:   6. Validates output, writes atomically (temp + mv)
79:   7. Adds Obsidian wikilink in project folder (no file copies)
80: EOF
81: }
82: 
83: while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
84:     case $1 in
85:     --worktree)
86:         WORKTREE_PATH="$2"
87:         shift 2
88:         ;;
89:     --cwd)
90:         PROJECT_CWD="$2"
91:         shift 2
92:         ;;
93:     --ticket)
94:         TICKET_ID="$2"
95:         shift 2
96:         ;;
97:     --session-id)
98:         FORCE_SESSION_ID="$2"
99:         shift 2
100:         ;;
101:     --reconcile)
102:         RECONCILE=true
103:         shift
104:         ;;
105:     --reason)
106:         SYNTH_REASON="$2"
107:         shift 2
108:         ;;
109:     --force)
110:         FORCE=true
111:         shift
112:         ;;
113:     --dry-run)
114:         DRY_RUN=true
115:         shift
116:         ;;
117:     --verbose)
118:         VERBOSE=true
119:         shift
120:         ;;
121:     --help | -h)
122:         show_help
123:         exit 0
124:         ;;
125:     *)
126:         echo -e "${RED}Error: Unknown option $1${NC}" >&2
127:         exit 1
128:         ;;
129:     esac
130: done
131: 
132: # Resolve working directory
133: if [[ -n "$WORKTREE_PATH" ]]; then
134:     PROJECT_CWD="$WORKTREE_PATH"
135: elif [[ -z "$PROJECT_CWD" ]]; then
136:     PROJECT_CWD="${PWD}"
137: fi
138: 
139: # Dedup gate: skip if same cwd was synthesised within DEDUP_SEC.
140: # Reconcile, dry-run, and --force always bypass. Stamp file is per-cwd hash.
141: if ! $FORCE && ! $DRY_RUN && ! $RECONCILE; then

148:         elapsed=$((now_epoch - last_sync_epoch))
149:         if [[ "$elapsed" -lt "$DEDUP_SEC" ]]; then
150:             echo -e "${YELLOW}Dedup: last sync ${elapsed}s ago (window ${DEDUP_SEC}s, reason=${SYNTH_REASON}), skipping. Use --force to bypass.${NC}" >&2
151:             exit 0
152:         fi
153:     fi
154:     touch "$DEDUP_STAMP"
155: fi
156: 
157: # Ensure Obsidian vault exists
158: if [[ ! -d "$OBSIDIAN_VAULT" ]]; then
159:     echo -e "${RED}Error: Obsidian vault not found at $OBSIDIAN_VAULT${NC}" >&2
160:     exit 1
161: fi
162: 
163: mkdir -p "$CLAUDE_SESSIONS_DIR"
164: 
165: # --- Utilities ---
166: 
167: slugify() {
168:     local text="$1"
169:     text=$(echo "$text" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]')
170:     text=$(echo "$text" | sed 's/[^a-z0-9 _-]//g')
171:     text=$(echo "$text" | sed 's/[ _]/-/g')
172:     text=$(echo "$text" | sed 's/-\{2,\}/-/g')
173:     text=$(echo "$text" | sed 's/^-//;s/-$//')
174:     echo "${text:0:60}"
175: }

#

236: def build_extraction_prompt(session: Dict[str, Any], triggers: List[Dict]) -> str:
237:     """Build the prompt for Claude to extract memories."""
238:     # Format transcript
239:     transcript_parts = []

241:     asst_msgs = session['assistant_messages']
242: 
243:     for i in range(max(len(user_msgs), len(asst_msgs))):
244:         if i < len(user_msgs):
245:             transcript_parts.append(f'USER: {user_msgs[i][:1000]}')
246:         if i < len(asst_msgs):
247:             transcript_parts.append(f'ASSISTANT: {asst_msgs[i][:500]}')
248: 
249:     transcript = '\n\n---\n\n'.join(transcript_parts)
250:     if len(transcript) > MAX_TRANSCRIPT_LENGTH:
251:         transcript = transcript[:MAX_TRANSCRIPT_LENGTH] + '\n\n[... truncated ...]'
252: 
253:     # Format triggers section
254:     triggers_section = ''
255:     if triggers:
256:         trigger_lines = []
257:         for i, t in enumerate(triggers[:10]):
258:             trigger_lines.append(

261:         triggers_section = f'''
262: ## Pre-Detected Triggers (HIGH PRIORITY)
263: {chr(10).join(trigger_lines)}

266: '''

268:     # Memory types documentation

271:         for t, info in MEMORY_TYPES.items()
272:     ])
273: 
274:     return f'''# Memory Extraction Task
275: 
276: Analyze this Claude Code session and extract memories worth remembering.
277: 
278: ## Session Info
279: - **Session ID:** {session['session_id'][:8]}
280: - **Work Type:** {session['work_type']}
281: - **Messages:** {len(session['user_messages'])} user, {len(session['assistant_messages'])} assistant
282: - **Triggers Detected:** {len(triggers)}
283: {triggers_section}
284: ## Session Transcript
285: ```
286: {transcript}
287: ```
288: 
289: ## Memory Types
290: {type_docs}
291: 
292: ## Output Format
293: Respond with ONLY a JSON array of memories:
294: 
295: ```json
296: [
297:   {{
298:     "type": "correction|decision|insight|learning|commitment|pattern|workflow",
299:     "category": "technical|systems|relationship|creative|planning",
300:     "title": "Brief title (5-10 words)",
301:     "summary": "1-2 sentence summary",
302:     "reasoning": "Why this is worth remembering",
303:     "confidence": 0.5-1.0,
304:     "entities": ["entity1", "entity2"],
305:     "context": "Relevant quote from transcript"
306:   }}
307: ]
308: ```
309: 
310: ## Rules
311: - Extract 0-{MAX_MEMORIES_PER_SESSION} memories maximum
312: - Quality over quantity
313: - Corrections require confidence >= 0.7
314: - Other types require confidence >= 0.5
315: - If no memories worth extracting, return: []'''
316: 
317: 
318: def call_claude_for_extraction(prompt: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
319:     """Call Claude CLI to extract memories from the prompt."""
320:     try:
321:         # Use claude CLI with print mode
322:         result = subprocess.run(
323:             ['claude', '--print', '-p', prompt],
324:             capture_output=True,
325:             text=True,
326:             timeout=120
327:         )
328: 
329:         if result.returncode != 0:
330:             return []
331: 
332:         response = result.stdout
333: 
334:         # Parse JSON from response
335:         json_match = (
336:             re.search(r'```json\n([\s\S]*?)\n```', response) or
337:             re.search(r'```\n([\s\S]*?)\n```', response) or
338:             re.search(r'\[[\s\S]*\]', response)
339:         )
340: 
341:         if not json_match:
342:             return []
343: 
344:         json_str = json_match.group(1) if json_match.lastindex else json_match.group(0)
345:         memories = json.loads(json_str)
346: 
347:         if not isinstance(memories, list):
348:             return []
349: 
350:         # Validate memories
351:         valid_memories = []
352:         for mem in memories:
353:             if validate_memory(mem):
354:                 valid_memories.append(mem)
355: 
356:         return valid_memories[:MAX_MEMORIES_PER_SESSION]
357: 
358:     except Exception as e:
359:         print(f'Error calling Claude: {e}', file=sys.stderr)
360:         return []
361: 
362: 
363: def validate_memory(mem: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
364:     """Validate a memory object has required fields and valid values."""
365:     if not isinstance(mem, dict):
366:         return False
367: 
368:     # Required fields
369:     required = ['type', 'title', 'summary', 'confidence']
370:     if not all(k in mem for k in required):
371:         return False
372: 
373:     # Valid type

375:         return False
376: 
377:     # Confidence threshold
378:     confidence = float(mem.get('confidence', 0))
379:     min_conf = MEMORY_TYPES[mem['type']]['min_confidence']
380:     if confidence < min_conf:
381:         return False
382: 
383:     return True
384: 
385: 
386: def main():
387:     """Main hook entry point."""
388:     # Read hook input from stdin
389:     try:
390:         input_data = json.loads(sys.stdin.read())
391:     except (json.JSONDecodeError, Exception):
392:         print(json.dumps({"continue": True}))
393:         return
394: 
395:     session_id = input_data.get('session_id', '')
396: 
397:     if not session_id:
398:         print(json.dumps({"continue": True}))
399:         return
400: 
401:     # Parse the session
402:     session = parse_session_jsonl(session_id)
403: 
404:     if not session or not session['user_messages']:
405:         print(json.dumps({"continue": True}))
406:         return
407: 
408:     # Check if we already have this session synced
409:     existing_session = find_session_by_id(session_id)
410:     if existing_session:
411:         # Check if memories already extracted
412:         content = existing_session.read_text()
413:         if 'memories_extracted: true' in content.lower():
414:             print(json.dumps({"continue": True}))
415:             return
416:         session_path = existing_session
417:     else:
418:         # Write session to Obsidian
419:         created = datetime.fromisoformat(session['created_at'].replace('Z', '+00:00')) if session['created_at'] else datetime.now()
420:         session_path = write_session(
421:             session_id=session['session_id'],
422:             created=created,
423:             work_type=session['work_type'],
424:             first_message=session['first_message'],
425:             last_message=session['last_message'],
426:             user_messages=session['user_messages'],
427:             assistant_messages=session['assistant_messages'],
428:             tool_calls=session['tool_calls'],
429:             files_touched=session['files_touched'],
430:             token_estimate=session['token_estimate']
431:         )
432: 
433:     if not session_path:
434:         print(json.dumps({"continue": True}))
435:         return
436: 
437:     # Detect triggers
438:     triggers = detect_triggers(session['user_messages'])
439: 
440:     # Build extraction prompt
441:     prompt = build_extraction_prompt(session, triggers)
442: 
443:     # Call Claude for extraction
444:     memories = call_claude_for_extraction(prompt)
445: 
446:     # Write memories to Obsidian
447:     session_rel_path = f"Claude/Sessions/{session_path.name}" if session_path else None
448:     memories_written = 0
449: 
450:     for mem in memories:
451:         result = write_memory(
452:             memory_type=mem['type'],

1: """
2: Ripgrep Search Utilities
3: 
4: Fast filesystem search using ripgrep (rg).
5: No database required - searches Obsidian vault directly.
6: """
7: 
8: import subprocess
9: import re
10: from pathlib import Path
11: from typing import List, Tuple, Optional, Dict, Any
12: from datetime import datetime, timedelta
13: 
14: from .config import (
15:     OBSIDIAN_VAULT, CLAUDE_DIR, MEMORIES_DIR, SESSIONS_DIR,
16:     MAX_ITEMS_PER_TYPE, STOP_WORDS
17: )
18: 
19: 
20: def run_ripgrep(
21:     pattern: str,
22:     path: Optional[Path] = None,
23:     flags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
24:     timeout: int = 5
25: ) -> List[str]:
26:     """
27:     Run ripgrep and return matching file paths.
28: 
29:     Args:
30:         pattern: Regex pattern to search for
31:         path: Directory to search in (defaults to vault)
32:         flags: Additional rg flags
33:         timeout: Command timeout in seconds
34: 
35:     Returns:
36:         List of matching file paths
37:     """
38:     cmd = ['rg', '-l', '--type', 'md']
39:     if flags:
40:         cmd.extend(flags)
41:     cmd.append(pattern)
42:     cmd.append(str(path or OBSIDIAN_VAULT))
43: 
44:     try:
45:         result = subprocess.run(
46:             cmd,
47:             capture_output=True,
48:             text=True,
49:             timeout=timeout
50:         )
51:         if result.returncode == 0:
52:             return [p.strip() for p in result.stdout.strip().split('\n') if p.strip()]
53:         return []
54:     except (subprocess.TimeoutExpired, FileNotFoundError):
55:         return []
56: 
57: 
58: def run_ripgrep_content(
59:     pattern: str,
60:     path: Optional[Path] = None,
61:     context_lines: int = 2,
62:     timeout: int = 5
63: ) -> List[Tuple[str, str]]:
64:     """
65:     Run ripgrep and return file paths with matching content snippets.
66: 
67:     Args:
68:         pattern: Regex pattern to search for
69:         path: Directory to search in (defaults to vault)
70:         context_lines: Lines of context around matches
71:         timeout: Command timeout in seconds
72: 
73:     Returns:
74:         List of (file_path, snippet) tuples
75:     """
76:     cmd = ['rg', '--type', 'md', '-H', '-n', f'-C{context_lines}']
77:     cmd.append(pattern)
78:     cmd.append(str(path or OBSIDIAN_VAULT))
79: 
80:     try:
81:         result = subprocess.run(
82:             cmd,
83:             capture_output=True,
84:             text=True,
85:             timeout=timeout
86:         )
87:         if result.returncode == 0:
88:             matches = []
89:             current_file = None
90:             current_content = []
91: 
92:             for line in result.stdout.split('\n'):
93:                 if ':' in line:
94:                     parts = line.split(':', 2)
95:                     if len(parts) >= 2:
96:                         file_path = parts[0]
97:                         if file_path != current_file:
98:                             if current_file and current_content:
99:                                 matches.append((current_file, '\n'.join(current_content[-5:])))
100:                             current_file = file_path
101:                             current_content = []
102:                         if len(parts) >= 3:
103:                             current_content.append(parts[2])
104: 
105:             if current_file and current_content:
106:                 matches.append((current_file, '\n'.join(current_content[-5:])))
107: 
108:             return matches[:10]
109:         return []
110:     except (subprocess.TimeoutExpired, FileNotFoundError):
111:         return []
112: 
113: 
114: def search_corrections(limit: int = MAX_ITEMS_PER_TYPE) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
115:     """
116:     Get recent corrections (always relevant, highest priority).
117: 
118:     Returns:
119:         List of correction memory dicts with title, summary, file
120:     """
121:     corrections_dir = MEMORIES_DIR / 'corrections'
122:     if not corrections_dir.exists():
123:         return []
124: 
125:     corrections = []
126:     files = sorted(corrections_dir.glob('*.md'), key=lambda f: f.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
127: 
128:     for file in files[:limit]:
129:         try:
130:             content = file.read_text()
131:             memory = parse_memory_file(content, file)
132:             if memory:
133:                 corrections.append(memory)
134:         except Exception:
135:             pass
136: 
137:     return corrections
138: 
139: 
140: def search_by_entity(entity: str, limit: int = MAX_ITEMS_PER_TYPE) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
141:     """
142:     Search memories that mention a specific entity.
143: 
144:     Args:
145:         entity: Entity name to search for (person, project, etc.)
146:         limit: Maximum results to return
147: 
148:     Returns:
149:         List of matching memory dicts
150:     """
151:     if not MEMORIES_DIR.exists():
152:         return []
153: 
154:     # Search for entity in frontmatter entities field or content
155:     files = run_ripgrep(entity, MEMORIES_DIR, ['-i'])
156: 
157:     memories = []
158:     seen_files = set()
159: 
160:     for file_path in files[:limit * 2]:  # Get extra to account for duplicates
161:         if file_path in seen_files:
162:             continue
163:         seen_files.add(file_path)
164: 
165:         try:
166:             content = Path(file_path).read_text()
167:             memory = parse_memory_file(content, Path(file_path))
168:             if memory:
169:                 memories.append(memory)
170:         except Exception:
171:             pass
172: 
173:         if len(memories) >= limit:
174:             break
175: 
176:     return memories
177: 
178: 
179: def search_by_keywords(
180:     keywords: List[str],
181:     limit: int = MAX_ITEMS_PER_TYPE
182: ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
183:     """
184:     Search memories by keywords from prompt.
185: 
186:     Args:
187:         keywords: List of keywords to search for
188:         limit: Maximum results to return
189: 
190:     Returns:
191:         List of matching memory dicts
192:     """
193:     if not MEMORIES_DIR.exists() or not keywords:
194:         return []
195: 
196:     memories = []
197:     seen_files = set()
198: 
199:     # Build OR pattern for ripgrep
200:     pattern = '|'.join(re.escape(k) for k in keywords[:5])
201: 
202:     files = run_ripgrep(pattern, MEMORIES_DIR, ['-i'])
203: 
204:     for file_path in files:
205:         if file_path in seen_files:
206:             continue
207:         seen_files.add(file_path)
208: 
209:         try:
210:             content = Path(file_path).read_text()
211:             memory = parse_memory_file(content, Path(file_path))
212:             if memory:
213:                 memories.append(memory)
214:         except Exception:
215:             pass
216: 
217:         if len(memories) >= limit:
218:             break
219: 
220:     return memories

640:     const text = result.stdout || result.stderr
641:     if (text) {
642:       output.context.push(text)
643:     }
644:   }
645: 
646:   return {
647:     event: async ({ event }) => {
648:       switch (event.type) {
649:         case "session.created": {
650:           currentSessionID = event.properties.info.id
651:           shutdownHandled = false
652:           sessionContext.clear()
653:           transientContext.length = 0
654:           seenPromptMessages.clear()
655:           messageRoles.clear()
656:           messageTexts.clear()
657:           messageOrder.length = 0
658:           lastBridgeReviewedAssistant = ""
659:           await runScript(tmuxHookPath("tmux-agent-start.sh"), undefined, {}, STARTUP_FAST_TIMEOUT_MS)
660:           void collectSessionStartContext().catch(() => {})
661:           break
662:         }
663: 
664:         case "message.updated": {
665:           const info = event.properties.info as { id?: string; role?: string; modelID?: string }
666:           if (info?.id) {
667:             rememberMessage(info.id, info.role)
668:           }
669:           if (typeof info?.modelID === "string" && info.modelID) {
670:             currentOpenCodeModel = info.modelID
671:           }
672:           break
673:         }
674: 
675:         case "message.part.updated": {
676:           const { part } = event.properties
677:           if (part.type !== "text") {
678:             break
679:           }
680: 
681:           rememberMessage(part.messageID, undefined, part.text ?? "")
682: 
683:           if (messageRoles.get(part.messageID) !== "user") {
684:             break
685:           }
686: 
687:           if (seenPromptMessages.has(part.messageID)) {
688:             break
689:           }
690: 
691:           seenPromptMessages.add(part.messageID)
692:           const prompt = part.text ?? ""
693:           await Promise.all([

set -euo pipefail

if [ "${OPENCODE_JFDI_AUTO_SYNC:-1}" = "0" ]; then

parts.text:
**Planning process optimization**

I need to focus on creating a solid plan since the user wants to explore optimization options. I should clarify that there won’t be any edits in plan mode, and I can only implement changes after receiving approval. I’m wondering if I should suggest cleanup for processes like terminating stale operations. The root cause seems to stem from duplicate entries in settings, which could lead to recursive issues. This is quite serious, and I need to ensure we address it effectively.

**Optimizing session management**

I’m examining how the handleShutdown sync runs and noticing issues with session-synthesize and jfdi sync running together on deleted items. The tmux-open duplicates per pane are consuming CPU, and I suspect the rg command is linked to Obsidian, checking TODO links through a cache. My plan is to optimize it by adding locks in the hooks to prevent conflicts. I also want to ensure that the session doesn't invoke unnecessary commands by implementing environment guards.

**Improving memory extraction process**

I’m thinking about how to enhance the memory extraction process. I want to skip extraction if the first message starts with "# Memory Extraction Task" for safety. Additionally, I plan to implement a lock keyed by session_id to avoid concurrent sessions. It seems I could minimize calls to Claude by only extracting when there are actual triggers. But I should also consider whether the user wants general memories extracted, so implementing an environment flag to require triggers by default might be a good idea.

**Root Cause**
The duplicates are mostly agent lifecycle hooks, not btop displaying incorrectly.

Main offenders I found:

- Many `bash /Users/shahe.islam/dotfiles/scripts/hook-run.sh 120 python3 ~/.claude/hooks/jfdi/session-end-extract.py`
- Several `claude --print -p # Memory Extraction Task...`
- Multiple `session-synthesize.sh` / Obsidian sync processes
- Multiple `tmux-open.sh` + `ocv attach` processes, one per active OpenCode/tmux pane
- `rg ... /Users/shahe.islam/obsidian` searches from Obsidian/session-memory indexing

The biggest actual bug is recursive memory extraction: `session-end-extract.py` calls `claude --print`, and that nested Claude invocation appears to trigger hooks again. The process list showed a `claude --print` prompt containing nested `# Memory Extraction Task` prompts, which means the extractor is extracting memories from its own extraction sessions.

**Optimisation Plan**
I can make this much lighter with small changes:

1. Add a recursion guard to `session-end-extract.py` so nested `claude --print` runs do not trigger memory extraction again.
2. Add a defensive skip when the transcript itself starts with `# Memory Extraction Task`.
3. Add a per-session lock/TTL so multiple Stop/SessionEnd hooks cannot extract the same session concurrently.
4. Remove or reduce `--force` on `session-synthesize.sh` where it bypasses the existing dedup gate.
5. Optionally make OpenCode shutdown sync lighter by limiting auto-extract/synthesis unless explicitly enabled.

**Expected Result**
This should reduce the duplicate `bash`, `python`, `claude --print`, and Obsidian `rg` bursts significantly. The remaining `ocv attach` / `tmux-open.sh` entries are normal if you have multiple OpenCode panes running, but we can also audit for stale panes after the hook recursion is fixed.

I’m currently in read-only plan mode, so I haven’t changed or killed anything. If you approve, I’ll implement the guards and run the dotfiles hook tests.
Is it worth getting rid of these processes that are eating up so much memory altogether?

**Considering the answer approach**

I need to find a solution. It seems we're in read-only mode, and I wonder if I should consider disabling some restrictions. Maybe I need to inspect things more closely. Although I think we have enough information already, the question might require a nuanced answer. I'm questioning whether tools are necessary in this situation, but I’ll have to think about how to proceed.

Yes, partly. But I would not remove all of them.

The memory shown in btop is misleading in two ways:

- Many `bash` wrappers are short-lived hook parents and will disappear naturally.
- macOS reports resident/shared memory per process, so totals can look worse than real unique memory.

The ones worth eliminating or reducing are:

- `session-end-extract.py` spawning `claude --print`: yes. This is expensive and currently appears recursive. It should be guarded or disabled by default.
- Obsidian-wide `rg` searches: yes. These should be deduped/throttled, especially on Stop/SessionEnd.
- Duplicate `session-synthesize.sh` runs: yes. Remove `--force` where it bypasses dedup, or combine Stop/SessionEnd synthesis into one path.
- Repeated `entire hooks ... stop/session-end`: maybe. Keep if useful, but it should be lightweight and deduped.
- `ocv attach` / `tmux-open.sh`: probably keep. These are your live OpenCode panes; killing them closes active agent sessions. Only stale ones should be cleaned up.

My recommendation:

1. Disable recursive JFDI memory extraction first.
2. Keep session synthesis, but make it deduped and non-forced.
3. Keep tmux/OpenCode processes unless they are stale.
4. Add an opt-out env var for heavy end-of-session hooks, e.g. `JFDI_SESSION_EXTRACT_DISABLE=1` or `OPENCODE_JFDI_AUTO_SYNC=0`.

If you want maximum performance over automatic memory capture, the best tradeoff is to disable automatic `session-end-extract.py` entirely and run memory extraction manually/on schedule. That will remove the biggest CPU/memory spikes.
If we ran it manually on schedule once a day would that be okay? Would the session informatio even for sessions that have been closed persist?

**Exploring persistence options**

I need to figure out the best way to explain persistence. I think I could use existing knowledge, but it might work better to read scripts for reconciliation. I've already looked into session-synthesize, which includes reconciliation, so that’s interesting. It seems like session-end-extract might only work with session IDs and not in batches? Although, there’s session-distill-batch. I guess inspecting these options will give me clarity on the best approach!
I’ll verify the existing batch/reconcile scripts so the answer is based on how your dotfiles currently persist and recover closed sessions.

<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env bash
2: #
3: # session-distill-batch.sh - Batch memory distillation for unprocessed Obsidian session files
4: #
5: # Scans ~/obsidian/Claude/Sessions/ for session files that haven't had memory
6: # extraction run yet, then invokes session-end-extract.py for each one by
7: # piping the session_id via stdin JSON (matching the SessionEnd hook interface).
8: #
9: # Frontmatter formats handled:
10: #   New (jfdi hook output): memories_extracted: false/true, session_id field
11: #   Old (pre-jfdi format):  memory_extracted: 0/1, id field
12: #
13: # Performance: uses grep -l for bulk "already extracted" detection and comm(1)
14: # to compute the unprocessed set — no per-file subshell forks during scanning.
15: #
16: # Usage:
17: #   session-distill-batch.sh [--limit N] [--dry-run] [--priority] [--verbose]
18: #
19: # Flags:
20: #   --limit N     Process at most N sessions (default: 10)
21: #   --dry-run     List files that would be processed, no side effects
22: #   --priority    Order by work_type: debugging > development > research > planning > general
23: #   --verbose     Show per-file detail
24: 
25: set -euo pipefail
26: 
27: # ─── Configuration ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28: 
29: OBSIDIAN_VAULT="${OBSIDIAN_VAULT:-$HOME/obsidian}"
30: SESSIONS_DIR="$OBSIDIAN_VAULT/Claude/Sessions"
31: EXTRACTOR="$HOME/.claude/hooks/jfdi/session-end-extract.py"
32: LOG_DIR="$HOME/.claude/hooks/logs"
33: LOG_FILE="$LOG_DIR/distill-batch-$(date +%Y-%m-%d).log"
34: 
35: # ─── Defaults ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
36: 
37: LIMIT=10
38: DRY_RUN=false
39: PRIORITY=false
40: VERBOSE=false
41: 
42: # ─── Argument Parsing ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43: 
44: show_help() {
45:     cat <<'EOF'
46: session-distill-batch.sh - Batch memory distillation for Obsidian sessions
47: 
48: USAGE:
49:   session-distill-batch.sh [OPTIONS]
50: 
51: OPTIONS:
52:   --limit N     Process at most N unprocessed sessions (default: 10)
53:   --dry-run     List files that would be processed, no extraction
54:   --priority    Order by work_type importance before processing
55:                   debugging > development > research > planning > general
56:   --verbose     Show per-file detail including session IDs
57:   --help, -h    Show this help
58: 
59: ENVIRONMENT:
60:   OBSIDIAN_VAULT  Path to Obsidian vault (default: ~/obsidian)
61: 
62: WHAT IT DOES:
63:   1. Scans via grep -l for extracted files (bulk, single pass — fast)
64:   2. Uses comm(1) to diff all non-synth .md vs extracted set
65:   3. Candidates = non-synth sessions not in extracted set
66:   4. Extracts session_id from frontmatter (new: session_id field, old: id field)
67:   5. Pipes {"session_id": "..."} JSON to session-end-extract.py stdin
68:   6. On clean exit (0), updates frontmatter to set memories_extracted: true
69:   7. Reports counts: processed / skipped (already done) / failed
70: 
71: LOG:
72:   ~/.claude/hooks/logs/distill-batch-YYYY-MM-DD.log
73: EOF
74: }
75: 
76: while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
77:     case $1 in
78:     --limit)
79:         LIMIT="$2"
80:         shift 2
81:         ;;
82:     --dry-run)
83:         DRY_RUN=true
84:         shift
85:         ;;
86:     --priority)
87:         PRIORITY=true
88:         shift
89:         ;;
90:     --verbose)
91:         VERBOSE=true
92:         shift
93:         ;;
94:     --help | -h)
95:         show_help
96:         exit 0
97:         ;;
98:     *)
99:         echo "Error: Unknown option: $1" >&2
100:         echo "Run with --help for usage." >&2
101:         exit 1
102:         ;;
103:     esac
104: done
105: 
106: # ─── Validation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
107: 
108: if [[ ! -d "$SESSIONS_DIR" ]]; then
109:     echo "Error: Sessions directory not found: $SESSIONS_DIR" >&2
110:     exit 1
111: fi
112: 
113: if [[ ! -f "$EXTRACTOR" ]]; then
114:     echo "Error: Extractor not found: $EXTRACTOR" >&2
115:     exit 1
116: fi
117: 
118: # ─── Logging ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
119: 
120: log() {
121:     local msg
122:     msg="[$(date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S')] $*"
123:     echo "$msg"
124:     if [[ "$DRY_RUN" != "true" ]]; then
125:         mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
126:         echo "$msg" >>"$LOG_FILE"
127:     fi
128: }
129: 
130: vlog() {
131:     if [[ "$VERBOSE" == "true" ]]; then
132:         log "$@"
133:     fi
134: }
135: 
136: # ─── Bulk Candidate Detection ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
137: #
138: # Strategy: compute candidates as a set difference using text stream tools.
139: #   all_non_synth  = ls | grep -v synth | sort
140: #   extracted      = grep -ril 'memories_extracted: true' + grep -ril 'memory_extracted: 1' | sort -u
141: #   candidates     = comm -23 all_non_synth extracted
142: #
143: # This avoids forking a subprocess per file — the whole scan is O(n) I/O
144: # with a handful of processes regardless of directory size.
145: #
146: # Temp files are used for comm (which requires seekable sorted input).
147: # They are cleaned up via a trap on EXIT.
148: 
149: _TMP_ALL=""
150: _TMP_EXTRACTED=""
151: 
152: cleanup_tmpfiles() {
153:     [[ -n "$_TMP_ALL" ]] && rm -f "$_TMP_ALL"
154:     [[ -n "$_TMP_EXTRACTED" ]] && rm -f "$_TMP_EXTRACTED"
155: }
156: trap cleanup_tmpfiles EXIT
157: 
158: build_file_sets() {
159:     _TMP_ALL=$(mktemp)
160:     _TMP_EXTRACTED=$(mktemp)
161: 
162:     # Choose search tool: rg is significantly faster than grep -ril on macOS HFS+.
163:     local search_cmd
164:     if command -v rg &>/dev/null; then
165:         search_cmd="rg_search"
166:     else
167:         search_cmd="grep_search"
168:     fi
169: 
170:     rg_search() {
171:         local pattern="$1"
172:         rg -l "$pattern" "$SESSIONS_DIR" --type md 2>/dev/null || true
173:     }
174: 
175:     grep_search() {
176:         local pattern="$1"
177:         grep -ril "$pattern" "$SESSIONS_DIR" --include='*.md' 2>/dev/null || true
178:     }
179: 
180:     # Run all three scans in parallel using background jobs to minimize wall time.

(Showing lines 1-180 of 492. Use offset=181 to continue.)
</content>

parts.text: The existing code already supports deferred processing: raw Claude JSONL lives under `~/.claude/projects`, synthesized session notes live under `~/obsidian/Claude/Sessions`, and there is a batch distillation script for unprocessed sessions.

Yes, once daily is a good tradeoff.

Closed session data should persist because Claude stores raw session JSONL files under:

`~/.claude/projects/<project-slug>/<session-id>.jsonl`

Your dotfiles also write synthesized session notes to:

`~/obsidian/Claude/Sessions/`

Existing support I found:

- `scripts/obsidian/session-synthesize.sh --reconcile` catches up missed/closed sessions from recent JSONL files.
- `scripts/obsidian/session-distill-batch.sh` scans `~/obsidian/Claude/Sessions/` for sessions without `memories_extracted: true` and processes them later.
- `session-end-extract.py` can process a session later if given `{"session_id": "..."}` on stdin.

Caveat: `session-synthesize.sh --reconcile` currently looks at JSONL files modified in the last 7 days, so daily is fine. If you wanted weekly/monthly catch-up, I’d increase that window.

Recommended setup:

- Disable automatic `session-end-extract.py` on every Stop hook.
- Keep or dedupe lightweight session synthesis, or run `session-synthesize.sh --reconcile` daily too.
- Run daily batch extraction with a modest limit, e.g. `scripts/obsidian/session-distill-batch.sh --limit 10 --priority`.
- Add recursion/concurrency guards before relying on batch mode, otherwise the daily job can still spawn nested `claude --print`.

So yes: sessions should persist after closing, and daily scheduled extraction is likely better for your machine than doing it live on every agent stop.

 #!/usr/bin/env python3
 """
 SessionEnd Hook - Extract memories from completed Claude Code session.
 
 This hook triggers when a Claude Code session ends and:

 4. Writes memories directly to Obsidian vault

   "hook_type": "SessionEnd",
   "session_id": "abc123",
   "cwd": "/path/to/project",
   "transcript_path": "/path/to/transcript.txt"
 }
 
 Hook Output (stdout JSON):
 {
   "continue": true
 }

+LOCK_TTL_SECONDS = int(os.environ.get("JFDI_SESSION_EXTRACT_LOCK_TTL", "600"))
+
+
+def extraction_disabled() -> bool:
+    """Return true when extraction is explicitly disabled for this process tree."""
+    return os.environ.get("JFDI_SESSION_EXTRACT_DISABLE", "").lower() in {"1", "true", "yes"}
+
+
+def is_extraction_session(session: Dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
+    """Avoid extracting memories from the extractor's own Claude --print sessions."""
+    first_message = session.get("first_message", "").lstrip()
+    return first_message.startswith("# Memory Extraction Task")
+
+
+def acquire_session_lock(session_id: str) -> Optional[Path]:
+    """Acquire a best-effort per-session lock to prevent Stop/SessionEnd fan-out."""
+    safe_session_id = re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z0-9_.-]", "_", session_id)
+    LOCK_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    lock_path = LOCK_DIR / f"{safe_session_id}.lock"
+
+    try:
+        fd = os.open(str(lock_path), os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | os.O_WRONLY, 0o600)
+        with os.fdopen(fd, "w") as f:
+            f.write(f"{os.getpid()}\n{int(time.time())}\n")
+        return lock_path
+    except FileExistsError:
+        try:
+            age = time.time() - lock_path.stat().st_mtime
+            if age > LOCK_TTL_SECONDS:
+                lock_path.unlink()
+                return acquire_session_lock(session_id)
+        except OSError:
+            return None
+        return None
+
+
+def release_session_lock(lock_path: Optional[Path]) -> None:
+    if not lock_path:
+        return
+    try:
+        lock_path.unlink()
+    except OSError:
+        pass
+

Index: scripts/obsidian/daily-session-maintenance.sh
===================================================================
--- scripts/obsidian/daily-session-maintenance.sh	
+++ scripts/obsidian/daily-session-maintenance.sh	
@@ -0,0 +1,64 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Reconcile closed Claude sessions and distill memories in one scheduled batch.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+OBSIDIAN_VAULT="${OBSIDIAN_VAULT:-$HOME/obsidian}"
+DISTILL_LIMIT="${CLAUDE_DAILY_DISTILL_LIMIT:-10}"
+LOG_DIR="$HOME/.claude/hooks/logs"
+LOCK_DIR="${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/claude-daily-session-maintenance"
+LOCK_FILE="$LOCK_DIR/lock"
+LOCK_TTL_SECONDS="${CLAUDE_DAILY_SESSION_MAINTENANCE_LOCK_TTL:-21600}"
+
+log() {
+    printf '[%s] %s\n' "$(date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S')" "$*"
+}
+
+acquire_lock() {
+    mkdir -p "$LOCK_DIR"
+    if mkdir "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
+        printf '%s\n' "$$" >"$LOCK_FILE/pid"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    local lock_age
+    lock_age=$(($(date +%s) - $(stat -f %m "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null || stat -c %Y "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)))
+    if [[ "$lock_age" -gt "$LOCK_TTL_SECONDS" ]]; then
+        rm -rf "$LOCK_FILE"
+        mkdir "$LOCK_FILE"
+        printf '%s\n' "$$" >"$LOCK_FILE/pid"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+release_lock() {
+    rm -rf "$LOCK_FILE"
+}
+
+main() {
+    mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR"
+
+    if [[ ! -d "$OBSIDIAN_VAULT" ]]; then
+        log "Obsidian vault not found: $OBSIDIAN_VAULT"
+        exit 0
+    fi
+
+    if ! acquire_lock; then
+        log "Daily session maintenance already running; skipping"
+        exit 0
+    fi
+    trap release_lock EXIT
+
+    log "Reconciling recent Claude session notes"
+    bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/session-synthesize.sh" --reconcile
+
+    log "Distilling unprocessed session memories (limit=$DISTILL_LIMIT)"
+    bash "$SCRIPT_DIR/session-distill-batch.sh" --limit "$DISTILL_LIMIT" --priority
+
+    log "Daily session maintenance complete"
+}
+
+main "$@"

Index: scripts/obsidian/install-daily-session-maintenance.sh
===================================================================
--- scripts/obsidian/install-daily-session-maintenance.sh	
+++ scripts/obsidian/install-daily-session-maintenance.sh	
@@ -0,0 +1,101 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Install the daily Claude session reconcile/distillation LaunchAgent.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+LABEL="com.user.claude-daily-session-maintenance"
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+TEMPLATE_FILE="$SCRIPT_DIR/${LABEL}.plist.template"
+DOTFILES_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
+AGENTS_DIR="$HOME/Library/LaunchAgents"
+PLIST_FILE="$AGENTS_DIR/${LABEL}.plist"
+LOG_FILE="$HOME/Library/Logs/claude-daily-session-maintenance.log"
+GUI_DOMAIN="gui/$(id -u)"
+LOAD_AGENT=true
+ACTION="install"
+
+show_help() {
+    cat <<EOF
+install-daily-session-maintenance.sh - Install daily Claude session maintenance
+
+USAGE:
+  install-daily-session-maintenance.sh [--no-load]
+  install-daily-session-maintenance.sh --uninstall
+
+OPTIONS:
+  --no-load     Install/update plist but do not load it into launchctl
+  --uninstall   Boot out and remove the LaunchAgent plist
+  --help        Show this help
+EOF
+}
+
+bootout_agent() {
+    launchctl bootout "$GUI_DOMAIN" "$PLIST_FILE" >/dev/null 2>&1 ||
+        launchctl bootout "$GUI_DOMAIN/$LABEL" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+}
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+    --no-load)
+        LOAD_AGENT=false
+        shift
+        ;;
+    --uninstall)
+        ACTION="uninstall"
+        shift
+        ;;
+    --help | -h)
+        show_help
+        exit 0
+        ;;
+    *)
+        echo "Error: Unknown option $1" >&2
+        show_help >&2
+        exit 1
+        ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+if [[ "$ACTION" == "uninstall" ]]; then
+    bootout_agent
+    rm -f "$PLIST_FILE"
+    echo "Uninstalled: $LABEL"
+    exit 0
+fi
+
+if [[ ! -f "$TEMPLATE_FILE" ]]; then
+    echo "Error: Template not found: $TEMPLATE_FILE" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+if [[ ! -f "$DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/obsidian/daily-session-maintenance.sh" ]]; then
+    echo "Error: daily-session-maintenance.sh not found" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+mkdir -p "$AGENTS_DIR" "$(dirname "$LOG_FILE")"
+
+sed \
+    -e "s|__HOME__|${HOME}|g" \
+    -e "s|__DOTFILES_ROOT__|${DOTFILES_ROOT}|g" \
+    "$TEMPLATE_FILE" >"$PLIST_FILE"
+chmod 644 "$PLIST_FILE"
+
+if ! plutil -lint "$PLIST_FILE" >/dev/null; then
+    echo "Error: generated plist failed validation: $PLIST_FILE" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+echo "Installed: $PLIST_FILE"
+
+if $LOAD_AGENT; then
+    bootout_agent
+    launchctl bootstrap "$GUI_DOMAIN" "$PLIST_FILE"
+    launchctl enable "$GUI_DOMAIN/$LABEL" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+    echo "Loaded LaunchAgent: $LABEL"
+else
+    echo "Installed but not loaded (--no-load)."
+fi
+
+echo "Schedule: daily at 09:30 local time"
+echo "Manual run: bash $DOTFILES_ROOT/scripts/obsidian/daily-session-maintenance.sh"

Index: scripts/setup.sh
===================================================================
--- scripts/setup.sh	
+++ scripts/setup.sh	
@@ -1,2836 +1,2846 @@
 #!/usr/bin/env bash
 
 # setup.sh - Universal Cross-Platform Dotfiles Setup
 # Works on macOS and Linux with online/offline modes and flexible profiles
 
 set -euo pipefail
 
 # ============================================================================
 # Configuration
 # ============================================================================
 
 SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
 DOTFILES_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd)"
 
 # Default values
 PROFILE="${PROFILE:-standard}"
 OS="${OS:-auto}"
 MODE="${MODE:-auto}"
 DRY_RUN=false
 NO_CONFIRM=false
 VERBOSE=false
 SKIP_PACKAGES=false
 SKIP_DOTFILES=false
 SKIP_SHELLS=false
 SKIP_FONTS_APPS=false
 ENABLE_CLAUDE_CODE_BACKUP="${ENABLE_CLAUDE_CODE_BACKUP:-true}"
 ENABLE_CLAUDE_HEAVY_SETUP="${ENABLE_CLAUDE_HEAVY_SETUP:-false}"
 
 # ============================================================================
 # Help
 # ============================================================================
 
 show_help() {
     cat <<EOF
 Universal Dotfiles Setup - Cross-Platform Installation
 
 USAGE:
     $0 [OPTIONS]
 
 OPTIONS:
     --profile <name>           Installation profile (minimal|standard|comprehensive|dev|ops)
     --os <type>                OS type (auto|macos|linux) - default: auto-detect
     --mode <mode>              Installation mode (auto|online|offline) - default: auto-detect
     --offline-package <path>   Path to offline package for offline installation
 
     --dry-run                  Preview actions without executing
     --no-confirm               Skip confirmation prompts
     --verbose                  Show detailed output
 
     --skip-packages            Skip package installation
     --skip-dotfiles            Skip dotfiles symlinking
     --skip-shells              Skip shell configuration
     --skip-fonts-apps          Skip fonts and GUI applications (macOS)
     --clean                    Clear completion state to re-run all phases
 
     -h, --help                 Show this help message
 
 PROFILES:
     minimal         Essential tools only (fastest)
     standard        Balanced installation (default)
     comprehensive   Everything installed
     dev             Development-focused tools
     ops             DevOps/SRE focused tools
 
 EXAMPLES:
     # Auto-detect everything, standard profile
     $0
 
     # Minimal installation on Linux
     $0 --profile minimal --os linux
 
     # Comprehensive development setup
     $0 --profile comprehensive
 
     # Offline installation
     $0 --mode offline --offline-package ~/dotfiles-offline.tar.gz
 
     # Dry run to preview
     $0 --profile dev --dry-run
 
     # Automated installation (CI/scripts)
     $0 --profile minimal --no-confirm
 
     # Enable optional features (Nix, Pulse, Pi-Hole, Self-Hosted LLM, SonarQube)
     ENABLE_NIX=true ENABLE_PULSE=true ENABLE_PIHOLE=true ENABLE_SELFHOST_LLM=true ENABLE_SONARQUBE=true $0 --profile comprehensive
 
     # Enable Claude Code heavy backup ecosystem (OpenCode-first setups skip this by default)
     ENABLE_CLAUDE_HEAVY_SETUP=true $0 --profile comprehensive
 
     # Clone personal repositories (set environment variables)
     OBSIDIAN_REPO=git@github.com:user/obsidian.git \\
     NVIM_REPO=git@github.com:user/nvim.git \\
     OPENCODE_VIM_REPO=git@github.com:shaheislam/opencode-vim.git \\
     $0 --profile standard
 
 ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES:
     ENABLE_NIX=true         Enable Nix package manager installation (Phase 11)
     ENABLE_PULSE=true       Enable Pulse coding tracker installation (Phase 11)
     ENABLE_PIHOLE=true      Enable Pi-hole DNS ad blocker via Colima + Docker (Phase 11)
     ENABLE_SELFHOST_LLM=true  Enable self-hosted LLM stack (Ollama + Open WebUI) (Phase 11)
     ENABLE_SONARQUBE=true   Enable SonarQube code quality server via Colima + Docker (Phase 11)
     ENABLE_CLAUDE_CODE_BACKUP=true  Install/verify thin Claude Code CLI backup (Phase 4, default true)
     ENABLE_CLAUDE_HEAVY_SETUP=true  Enable Claude MCPs/plugins/LSPs/router/Recall/Agent Teams (Phase 4, default false)
     OBSIDIAN_REPO=<url>     Clone Obsidian vault from repository (Phase 10)
     NVIM_REPO=<url>         Clone personal Neovim config (Phase 10)
     OPENCODE_VIM_REPO=<url> Clone OpenCode Vim fork to ~/opencode-vim (Phase 10, default: git@github.com:shaheislam/opencode-vim.git; set empty to skip)
 
 For more information: https://github.com/shaheislam/dotfiles
 
 EOF
 }
 
 # ============================================================================
 # Parse Arguments
 # ============================================================================
 
 parse_args() {
     while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
         case $1 in
         --profile)
             [[ -z "${2:-}" ]] && {
                 echo "Error: --profile requires a value"
                 exit 1
             }
             PROFILE="$2"
             shift 2
             ;;
         --os)
             [[ -z "${2:-}" ]] && {
                 echo "Error: --os requires a value"
                 exit 1
             }
             OS="$2"
             shift 2
             ;;
         --mode)
             [[ -z "${2:-}" ]] && {
                 echo "Error: --mode requires a value"
                 exit 1
             }
             MODE="$2"
             shift 2
             ;;
         --offline-package)
             [[ -z "${2:-}" ]] && {
                 echo "Error: --offline-package requires a value"
                 exit 1
             }
             export OFFLINE_PACKAGE="$2"
             MODE="offline"
             shift 2
             ;;
         --dry-run)
             DRY_RUN=true
             shift
             ;;
         --no-confirm)
             NO_CONFIRM=true
             shift
             ;;
         --verbose)
             VERBOSE=true
             shift
             ;;
         --skip-packages)
             SKIP_PACKAGES=true
             shift
             ;;
         --skip-dotfiles)
             SKIP_DOTFILES=true
             shift
             ;;
         --skip-shells)
             SKIP_SHELLS=true
             shift
             ;;
         --skip-fonts-apps)
             SKIP_FONTS_APPS=true
             shift
             ;;
         --clean)
             CLEAN_STATE=true
             shift
             ;;
         -h | --help)
             show_help
             exit 0
             ;;
         *)
             echo "Unknown option: $1"
             show_help
             exit 1
             ;;
         esac
     done
 
     # Validate --os value
     if [[ "$OS" != "auto" ]]; then
         case "$OS" in
         macos | linux | wsl) ;;
         *)
             echo "Error: Invalid --os value '$OS'. Must be: auto, macos, linux, wsl"
             exit 1
             ;;
         esac
     fi
 
     # Validate --mode value
     if [[ "$MODE" != "auto" ]]; then
         case "$MODE" in
         online | offline) ;;
         *)
             echo "Error: Invalid --mode value '$MODE'. Must be: auto, online, offline"
             exit 1
             ;;
         esac
     fi
 
     # Export for child modules
     export PROFILE DRY_RUN NO_CONFIRM VERBOSE SKIP_PACKAGES SKIP_DOTFILES SKIP_SHELLS SKIP_FONTS_APPS
     export ENABLE_CLAUDE_CODE_BACKUP ENABLE_CLAUDE_HEAVY_SETUP
     export DOTFILES_ROOT SCRIPT_DIR
 }
 
 # ============================================================================
 # Load Modules
 # ============================================================================
 
 load_modules() {
     # Load common utilities
     # shellcheck source=./lib/common.sh
     source "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/common.sh"
 
     # Load package manager
     # shellcheck source=./lib/package-manager.sh
     source "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/package-manager.sh"
 
     # Load other modules
     # shellcheck source=./lib/binary-installer.sh
     source "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/binary-installer.sh"
 
     # shellcheck source=./lib/shell-setup.sh
     source "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/shell-setup.sh"
 
     # shellcheck source=./lib/dotfiles-manager.sh
     source "$SCRIPT_DIR/lib/dotfiles-manager.sh"
 }
 
 ensure_symlink_target() {
     local source_path="$1"
     local target_path="$2"
 
     mkdir -p "$(dirname "$target_path")"
 
     if [[ -L "$target_path" ]] && [[ "$(readlink "$target_path")" == "$source_path" ]]; then
         return 0
     fi
 
     rm -rf "$target_path"
     ln -s "$source_path" "$target_path"
 }
 
 sync_managed_file() {
     local source_path="$1"
     local target_path="$2"
 
     mkdir -p "$(dirname "$target_path")"
 
     if [[ -f "$target_path" ]] && cmp -s "$source_path" "$target_path"; then
         return 0
     fi
 
     cp "$source_path" "$target_path"
 }
 
 materialize_launchagent_template() {
     local source_path="$1"
     local target_path="$2"
 
     [[ -f "$source_path" ]] || return 1
 
     mkdir -p "$(dirname "$target_path")"
     if [[ -L "$target_path" ]]; then
         rm -f "$target_path"
     fi
 
     local escaped_home escaped_dotfiles_root
     escaped_home=$(printf '%s\n' "$HOME" | sed 's/[\/&]/\\&/g')
     escaped_dotfiles_root=$(printf '%s\n' "$DOTFILES_ROOT" | sed 's/[\/&]/\\&/g')
     sed \
         -e "s/__HOME__/$escaped_home/g" \
         -e "s/__DOTFILES_ROOT__/$escaped_dotfiles_root/g" \
         "$source_path" >"$target_path"
 }
 
 install_launchagent_template() {
     local label="$1"
     local started_message="$2"
     local skipped_message="$3"
     local already_message="${4:-$label already loaded}"
     local source_path="$DOTFILES_ROOT/Library/LaunchAgents/$label.plist"
     local target_path="$HOME/Library/LaunchAgents/$label.plist"
 
     materialize_launchagent_template "$source_path" "$target_path" || return 1
 
     if launchctl list 2>/dev/null | grep -q "$label"; then
         log_verbose "$already_message"
         return 0
     fi
 
     launchctl bootstrap "gui/$(id -u)" "$target_path" 2>/dev/null &&
         print_success "$started_message" ||
         log_verbose "$skipped_message"
 }
 
 setup_opencode_shared_server() {
     [[ "$DETECTED_OS" != "macos" ]] && return 0
 
     print_step "Setting up OpenCode shared server..."
 
     local claude_subscription_plugin="$HOME/.bun/install/global/node_modules/opencode-with-claude/dist/index.js"
     if [[ ! -f "$claude_subscription_plugin" ]]; then
         print_warning "OpenCode Claude subscription plugin missing; run full setup without --skip-packages"
     fi
 
     if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
         print_warning "DRY RUN: Would install and start OpenCode shared server"
         return 0
     fi
 
     mkdir -p "$HOME/.local/state/opencode"
     install_launchagent_template \
         "com.dotfiles.opencode-serve" \
         "OpenCode shared server started" \
         "OpenCode shared server start skipped" \
         "OpenCode shared server already running" || true
 }
 
 ensure_macos_command_line_tools_current() {
     [[ "$OS" != "macos" ]] && return 0
 
     if ! command_exists softwareupdate; then
         log_verbose "softwareupdate unavailable; skipping Command Line Tools check"
         return 0
     fi
 
     if ! xcode-select -p >/dev/null 2>&1; then
         if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
             print_warning "DRY RUN: Would require Xcode Command Line Tools installation via 'xcode-select --install'"
             return 0
         fi
 
         print_error "Xcode Command Line Tools are not installed"
         print_warning "Install them with: xcode-select --install"
         return 1
     fi
 
     print_step "Checking macOS Command Line Tools..."
 
     local clt_label=""
     clt_label=$(softwareupdate --list 2>/dev/null | awk -F': ' '/^\* Label: Command Line Tools for Xcode / {print $2; exit}')
 
     if [[ -z "$clt_label" ]]; then
         print_success "Command Line Tools already current"
         return 0
     fi
 
     if [[ "$DRY_RUN" == "true" ]]; then
         print_warning "DRY RUN: Would install $clt_label"
         return 0
     fi
 
     print_step "Installing $clt_label..."
     if sudo softwareupdate --install "$clt_label"; then
         print_success "Installed $clt_label"
         return 0
     fi
 
     print_error "Failed to install $clt_label"
     print_warning "Update Command Line Tools from System Settings or rerun: sudo softwareupdate --install \"$clt_label\""
     return 1
 }
 
 # ============================================================================
 # Preflight Checks
 # ============================================================================
 
 preflight_checks() {
     print_header "Preflight Checks"
 
     # Clear state if --clean was passed
     if [[ "${CLEAN_STATE:-false}" == "true" ]]; then
         print_step "Clearing completion state (--clean)..."
         clear_state
         print_success "State cleared — all phases will re-run"
     fi
 
     # Check disk space
     if ! check_disk_space 500; then
         print_error "Insufficient disk space"
         exit 1
     fi
 
     # Check required commands
     if ! check_required_commands bash curl; then
         print_error "Missing required commands"
         exit 1
     fi
 
     # Check profile exists
     if [[ ! -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/profiles/$PROFILE.conf" ]]; then
         print_error "Profile not found: $PROFILE"
